findings.evidence: Rectangle.lean:690 `private theorem eq_of_sides {R S : GridRectangleBetween x y} (hleft : R.left = S.left) (hright : R.right = S.right) : R = S` — identical body via `obtain ⟨_,_,_,_,_,_⟩` + `rfl`.
findings.fix: Drop `private` on the original in Rectangle.lean (or add `@[ext]` to the `GridRectangleBetween` structure, which currently has none) and reuse it here instead of redeclaring.
findings.issue: `GridRectangleBetween.eq_of_sides` is an exact copy of the existing `private theorem eq_of_sides` at Rectangle.lean:690 (same statement and proof).

findings.evidence: Complex.lean:52-84 `transposeEquiv ... := Finsupp.domLCongr {toFun := GridState.transpose, ...}` vs diff:246-276 `rotateEquiv ... := Finsupp.domLCongr {toFun := GridState.rotate, ...}`; the `_single`/`_apply` lemma proofs are line-for-line the same.
findings.fix: Introduce a single general `GridChain.relabelEquiv (e : GridState n ≃ GridState n)` (and its `_single`/`_apply` lemmas) in Complex.lean and define both `transposeEquiv` and `rotateEquiv` as specializations.
findings.issue: `GridChain.rotateEquiv` parallels `GridChain.transposeEquiv` (Complex.lean:52): both wrap `Finsupp.domLCongr` around a GridState involution, with identical `rotateEquiv_single`/`rotateEquiv_apply` proofs mirroring `transposeEquiv_single`/`transposeEquiv_apply`.

summary: The rotation symmetry itself is new and reuses the existing rectangle/differential stack well, but the file copies the private helper `eq_of_sides` verbatim from Rectangle.lean and re-implements the `GridChain` relabeling as a parallel of `transposeEquiv`.
